componentDescription: Text is the typography component for rendering all text content with design system tokens. Use it instead of bare HTML tags (h1, p, span) to ensure consistent font sizes, weights, and line heights across the application.
features: Semantic variant system (heading.lg, heading.md, body.md, body.sm, etc.)
Polymorphic rendering via 'as' prop (h1-h6, p, span, div, label)
Inherits no extra margin by default

usageExamples.title: Page heading and body text
usageExamples.description: Heading and description text using design system variants.
usageExamples.code: Welcome back Here's what's happening with your account today.

usageExamples.title: Card with mixed typography
usageExamples.description: Card header with heading and label variants.
usageExamples.code:
<Card>
  <Text variant="heading.sm" as="h3">{user.name}</Text>
  <Text variant="body.sm" color="text.subtle">{user.email}</Text>
  <Text variant="label.md" color="text.secondary" style={{ marginTop: 8 }}>Joined {formatDate(user.createdAt)}</Text>
</Card>

variant.llmContext: Controls font size, weight, and line height via design tokens. Common variants: 'heading.lg' (page titles), 'heading.md' (section titles), 'heading.sm' (card titles), 'body.md' (standard body text), 'body.sm' (secondary text), 'label.md' (field labels), 'label.sm' (captions). Always use semantic variants — do not override font-size manually.
variant.propDescription: Typography variant from the design token system

as.llmContext: The HTML element to render. Separate semantic meaning from visual appearance: you can render a 'heading.md' variant as an h2 for correct DOM hierarchy, or as a div if used in non-heading contexts. Use 'h1'-'h6' for headings, 'p' for paragraphs, 'span' for inline text, 'label' for form labels.
as.propDescription: HTML element to render (h1-h6, p, span, div, label)

color.llmContext: Text color from design tokens. Use 'text.primary' for main content, 'text.subtle' or 'text.secondary' for supporting text, 'text.disabled' for inactive text. Avoid raw hex colors — always use token names.

align.llmContext: 'left' is the default for LTR text. Use 'center' for headings in empty states or hero sections. Use 'right' for numeric values in table cells. Avoid right-aligning body text.
